subject: iPhone battery draining rapidly after update
description: Battery drops from 100 % to under 50 % within a few hours of normal use; started right after the latest iOS update.

ts_steps.title: Check Battery Usage
ts_steps.action: Settings → Battery — review Last 24 Hours and Last 10 Days; identify apps with unusually high screen-on or background time.

ts_steps.title: Disable Background App Refresh
ts_steps.action: Settings → General → Background App Refresh — turn off for apps that do not need real-time updates; consider turning off entirely and re-enabling selectively.

ts_steps.title: Force restart
ts_steps.action: Press and quickly release Volume Up, then Volume Down, then press and hold Side button until Apple logo appears; allow post-update indexing to finish (may take 24–48 hours).

internal_information: Spotlight re-indexing and Photos face-scan after an OTA update cause heavy CPU use for 1–2 days. If drain persists beyond 48 hours, check Battery Health for degraded capacity. Not an MDM issue.
